prompt: You are in a room with 100 randomly chosen people. What is the probability (in %) that at least one of them shares YOUR specific birthday?
source: Standard independent-trials derivation, often paired with the birthday paradox to distinguish ‘any match’ from ‘match with you’. See Grinstead & Snell, Introduction to Probability, §3.1.
revealHeadline: This is NOT the birthday paradox.
revealExplanation: When the match must be with YOUR specific date, the pair-multiplication disappears. Each of the 100 people independently has a 364/365 chance of not sharing your birthday, and you need all 100 to miss. People who just learned the birthday paradox often overshoot this one badly.
